name: Google Nest Cam Wired (2nd Generation) Indoor Security Camera

specs.Night Vision: Night Sight with color night vision
specs.Storage: 3 hours free event history, Google Home Aware for 30 days

whatWorks: On-device AI processing sets the Nest Cam Wired apart — person, animal, and vehicle detection happens on the camera itself rather than in the cloud, reducing latency for alerts and enabling detection even during internet outages. The distinction between a person vs. a package vs. a vehicle in alerts is accurate and reduces notification fatigue.

Google Home integration is the deepest in the category — Nest Cam footage shows natively in the Google Home app, feeds into Assistant routines, and works with Nest Hub displays for at-a-glance live view. If you're already in the Google ecosystem, this camera slots in more seamlessly than any competitor.

Night Sight color night vision leverages computational photography (similar to Pixel Night Sight) to produce color images in conditions where most cameras show grainy black-and-white infrared. Porch lights and street lamps provide enough ambient light for genuinely useful color footage.

The 10-foot USB-C cable provides meaningful placement flexibility — more than the 6-foot cables shipped with most competing cameras, allowing ceiling mounting or positioning across a room without an extension cable.
worthKnowing: Free storage is extremely limited — just 3 hours of event history without a Google Home subscription. Meaningful storage requires Google Home Aware at $8/month (or bundled with Google One), which is among the more expensive subscription models in the category.

No microSD card slot means local storage is not an option. Nest Cam is cloud-dependent for any clip retention beyond the brief free event history. For buyers who want subscription-free storage, this camera is not a good fit.

Alexa integration is functional but limited compared to native Google Home use — you can pull up a live view on Alexa displays, but detection events and routines don't cross-integrate with Alexa the way they do within Google Home.

HDR video is good but the 1080p resolution means it doesn't offer the sharpness of higher-resolution competitors for identifying fine details at distance. For a camera at $129 CAD, 2K or better would be expected from newer entrants.

commonComplaints.title: 3 hours of free storage is not enough…
commonComplaints.body: Anything over 3 hours old is gone without paying for Aware. That's the most restrictive free tier of any camera I've tried. Should be at least 7 days like Arlo.

commonComplaints.title: No local storage option at all…
commonComplaints.body: No SD card slot means you're locked into the cloud subscription. Wyze gives you local storage for free. For a $129 camera I expected more flexibility.

commonComplaints.title: Pricey with the required subscription…
commonComplaints.body: $129 for the camera plus $8/month for Aware — that's $225 in year one. The hardware is great but the total cost of ownership needs to be in the pitch.

commonComplaints.title: Only 1080p at this price…
commonComplaints.body: Wyze does 2.5K for $39. Nest charges $129 for 1080p. The AI and Google integration are good but I expected higher resolution for the price in 2024.

verdict: The Google Nest Cam Wired is the best camera for deep Google Home integration and on-device AI detection — if you're in the Google ecosystem and use Google Home routines, Assistant, or Nest Hub displays, this camera integrates more seamlessly than any competitor. The cloud-dependent storage and subscription cost make it less appealing for buyers who want local storage or minimal ongoing fees.

realBuyers.quote: The person vs. vehicle vs. animal distinction in alerts is accurate. I only get notified for things that matter. Zero false alerts in three months.
I can say 'Hey Google, show me the front door camera' on my Nest Hub and it just works. Tighter integration than anything else I've tried.
Night Sight color vision on my porch is genuinely impressive. I can see jacket colors and hair color in pitch dark with just the streetlight.
10-foot cable meant I could mount it on the ceiling of my entryway and still reach the outlet. Every other camera I tried needed an extension.

sources.title: Google Nest Cam Review — The Verge

metaDescription: Google Nest Cam Wired review for Canada: on-device AI, Night Sight, Google Home integration. Honest on limited free storage and subscription cost. ~$129 CAD.
introText: The Google Nest Cam Wired delivers on-device AI person/vehicle/animal detection, Night Sight color night vision, and seamless Google Home integration in a wired indoor camera. Available on Amazon.ca (ASIN B09F1S4YTX) at around $129 CAD.
